body_text: HashMap<String, Double> map = new HashMap<>(); // initialize a HashMap
// add entries, next we can retrieve them
clean_text: HashMap Example Code import java.util.HashMap; import java.util.Map; class HashMapTest { public static void main(String [] args) { int hashcode; String key; double value; int index, capacity = 16; HashMap<String, Double> map = new HashMap<>(); // initialize a HashMap // add entries, next we can retrieve them for (Map.Entry<String, Double> e : map.entrySet()) { // enumerate key = e.getKey(); value = e.getValue(); hashcode = e.hashCode(); index = hashcode & (capacity-1); System.out.println("Key: "+key+" Value: "+value +" HashCode: "+hashcode+" Index: "+index); } }

bullets: LinkedList class is an implementation of the linked list data
LinkedList behaviour is that of a dynamic array that grows
The linked list is implemented as a doubly linked list data
There are 2 methods to create an instance of a LinkedList
body_text: structure where elements are linked using pointers and
(or contracts) in size as items are added or removed
structure allowing for efcient insertion and removal of items
1. Default constructor LinkedList() creates an empty linked list
instance that contains all the elements of a specified collection
col. The order of the items in the linked list is determined by
the iterator of the collection col

The order of the items in the linked list is determined by the iterator of the collection col 14 / 21 LinkedList class is an implementation of the linked list data LinkedList behaviour is that of a dynamic array that grows The linked list is implemented as a doubly linked list data There are 2 methods to create an instance of a LinkedList
